title: Tariff-Proof But Not China-Proof: The Geopolitics of India’s Pharma Power
gist: India's generics-heavy pharma industry dodged the Trump administration's April 2026 100% tariffs on branded drugs, but its dependence on China for inputs leaves it 'not China-proof' — a vulnerability for India and its Western partners. (Text is a truncated teaser ending in '[...]'.)

insights.claim: The Trump administration's April 2026 100 percent tariffs on branded pharmaceuticals hit patented medicines but spared the low-cost generics that are India's stock-in-trade, with generics and biosimilars to be reassessed only after a year.
insights.speaker: Soumya Bhowmick
insights.so_what: Indian generics avoiding US tariffs for now is a near-term relief for Indian drugmakers, but the one-year reassessment is a forward risk to monitor.
insights.quote: The duties hit patented medicines, not the low-cost generics that are India's stock-in-trade, and the White House said the treatment of generics and biosimilars would be reassessed only after a year.
insights.locator: 'When the United States' Trump administration unveiled'

insights.claim: India supplies close to 20 percent of the world's generic medicines by volume, and in 2022 Indian firms accounted for around 47 percent of all generic prescriptions dispensed in the United States.
insights.speaker: Soumya Bhowmick
insights.so_what: India's outsized share of US generic supply makes any disruption to its pharma inputs a US healthcare-cost and drug-shortage risk.
insights.quote: It supplies close to 20 percent of the world's generic medicines by volume, and in 2022, Indian firms accounted for around 47 percent of all generic prescriptions dispensed in the United States.
insights.locator: 'India is, by common description, the pharmacy of the world.'

insights.claim: Indian companies supplied more than half of all American prescriptions in five of the ten largest therapy areas, including hypertension, mental health, and lipid regulators.
insights.speaker: Soumya Bhowmick
insights.so_what: Deep dependence in key therapy areas means India's China-linked input vulnerability is also a Western supply-security problem.
insights.quote: Indian companies supplied more than half of all American prescriptions in five of the ten largest therapy areas, including hypertension, mental health and lipid regulators

insights.claim: India's pharma industry is 'not China-proof' and its China exposure is a problem not only for India but for its Western partners.
insights.speaker: Soumya Bhowmick
insights.so_what: Reliance on Chinese inputs (e.g. APIs) is the real structural risk to India's pharma power — a supply-chain de-risking theme to watch.
insights.quote: It is not, however, China-proof – and exposure is a problem not only for India, but for its Western partners.
insights.locator: 'For now, India's robust pharma industry looks tariff-proof.'
